subject: Sociology
title: Student Debt and Inequality
description: One-minute skill: Student Debt and Inequality.

hook.text: Americans owe $1.7 trillion in student loans. An entire generation started adult life in debt.

buildup.text: As tuition rose and public funding fell, students borrowed more. Debt became the price of a middle-class life.

discovery.text: Debt burdens fall hardest on first-generation and minority students, who borrow more and earn less after.

twist.text: Education was supposed to reduce inequality. When it requires debt, it can actually increase it instead.

climax.text: Student debt transforms education from a public good into a private gamble with unequal odds.

punchline.text: The degree helps, but the debt hurts.

quiz.question: Who is most burdened by student debt?
